material.label: the hypercore skill: compound moves over the verbs

material.body:
# viewer v2

Three zones. Left: the intent panel — pending endorsements first (amber, with
an endorse button that asks once before spending judgment), then open work,
all statements grouped by segment in canonical order, recent folds. Center:
the graph — each work is a box containing its operations (membership is
nesting; contains edges are not drawn), only combinators draw as labeled
edges, layout is deterministic so the mental map survives a reload, zoom is
buttons plus a gentle wheel. Right: details — statements show owner, links,
and the reverse index (bound by / produced by); operations show roles,
verdicts with grounds, and material, with inline bodies fetched on demand.

The server gained GET /api/material/ID and POST /api/endorse. Endorse is the
operator acting (endorsement.md): the machine never calls it.
material.label: viewer v2 design

material.body:
# the settlement

The operator decided on 2026-06-10, in conversation, to bind the alphabet
design: work nodes are operations (frame, gather, derive, generate, test,
commit); products are material on operation nodes; relations carry the
combinators (depends-on, tests, commits, reframes, decomposes-into);
roles live in props, and a commit's decide is the operator's,
non-delegable. Implementation landed as git commit 10b54ab; tests
wn_44b27c3f, wn_4559dcbf, wn_7aca437d, wn_ddb2f58c all pass.

Held open by design: synthesize/repair stay out (wn_6a9e4db2); test may
split into challenge/verdict (wn_efd59a7c). Decide from practice.
material.label: the settlement

material.body:
# the work loop speaks the operation alphabet

folded: 2026-06-10T08:49:39+00:00
fold when: work nodes are operations (frame, gather, derive, generate, test, commit), their products are material on those nodes, relations carry the combinators, and roles live in props

## commits
- the work loop speaks the alphabet: six operation kinds, combinators as relations, roles in props  (material: m_b2bc67b2)

## tests
- pass: roles land in props with kind defaults; a commit's decide cannot be delegated to the machine
- pass: work-add accepts only the six operations; legacy kinds are refused
- pass: work-fold's machine gate reads test verdicts and requires a commit as settlement; render and load round-trip survive
- pass: combinators wire as relations: tests, commits binds a generate only, reframes a frame only, depends-on, decomposes-into; path traverses them

## history
- generate: test may want to split into challenge and verdict; decide from practice, not theory
- generate: roles as props on each operation (propose, execute, judge, decide); decide on a commit is the operator's and cannot be delegated
- generate: work-fold's machine check reads test operations' verdicts instead of check nodes
- generate: relation types carry the combinators: depends-on as causal link, tests, commits, reframes, decomposes-into
- generate: synthesize and repair stay out until the primitive-vs-compound contest resolves; adopt only if real work keeps wanting them
- generate: replace work-add kinds step/candidate/check/result with the six operations; products become material on operation nodes, not node kinds
material.label: the work loop speaks the operation alphabet

material.body:
# statements leave the graph

folded: 2026-06-10T07:59:34+00:00
fold when: graph.json holds no statement or segment nodes; statements live in a git-tracked store with envelope (id, text, owner, segment, order); operations and work reference statements by id, and show answers the reverse direction (statement to referencing nodes); every existing verb, render, load, and the viewer work unchanged

## results
- statements live beside the graph: statements table + statements.json, nodes reference them via on/produces props, show answers the reverse index, all verbs and views unchanged

## checks
- pass: graph.json holds no statement or segment nodes (kinds: node, work, step, check, candidate, result)
- pass: show on a statement answers the reverse direction: bound work, producing work, links both ways, material via the bound work
- pass: verbs, render, load, ingest, traversals, and viewer verified in a throwaway copy: statement cycle round-trips byte-identical; work cycle folds with material on the work node; /api/graph serves graph plus store
- pass: statements.json holds all 51 statements with envelope: id, text, owner, segment, ord, links, created_at

## history
- step: one-time migration: 51 statements moved with envelope and 2 depends-on links; spawned-by relations became on/produces props; m_cca715e7 rehomed to w_423ff83b; statement and segment nodes deleted
- step: verbs rewired: statement verbs speak to the store; work-open binds by id; work-fold attaches material to the work node; render reads the store; load reads both snapshots
- step: statements table added to schema; store grows statement CRUD, dump/load, and the reverse index (statement_references)
material.label: statements leave the graph

props.grounds: CDP, 2026-06-10: the queue rendered 'awaiting your judgment (11)' with endorse rows; an operator-judged scratch test appeared in it and the recorded verdict drained the queue from 1 to 0

nodes.label: toolbar summary: counts of statements pending endorsement and open work
readability pass: larger and higher-contrast graph labels, calmer edge labels, panel typography and spacing tuned for reading

nodes carry their meaning: statement text and operation labels readable on the node itself, loud state (verdict glyphs, pending endorsement, open vs folded), and the causal story readable from the picture — layout follows dependency flow
graph.json holds no statement or segment nodes (kinds: node, work, step, check, candidate, result)
moves land in the open viewer as named feed entries and a flash, without a refresh

props.grounds: CDP, 2026-06-10: endpoint- and poll-driven reloads both produced named feed entries within two 3s ticks (test pass / statement amended / operator endorsed), each clickable to its node; arrivals flash via the visible stand-in when their work is closed

nodes.label: a verb run outside the page shows up in the open viewer without a refresh

props.grounds: CDP against a sandbox serve: add-statement from the CLI moved the toolbar count 9→10 within two 3s poll ticks; a window marker survived, so the page re-derived rather than reloaded; read-only verbs leave /api/version unchanged

nodes.label: verbs, render, load, ingest, traversals, and viewer verified in a throwaway copy: statement cycle round-trips byte-identical; work cycle folds with material on the work node; /api/graph serves graph plus store
operator opens the viewer and can tell machine-owned statements and open work apart at a glance

props.grounds: operator, 2026-06-10, in conversation: the nodes are still not very useful to see — they're just different colors. At-a-glance legibility not reached; shape and color encode ownership but a node's meaning still takes a click.

nodes.label: the operator watches an operation land live, sees the queue of what awaits their judgment, and records a verdict or endorsement in the viewer without touching the CLI

work-add accepts only the six operations; legacy kinds are refused

nodes.label: operator named the failures: the white background is hard on the eyes (wants dark mode), the UI needs further readability and UX work, and the view goes stale — changes require a manual refresh
theme as CSS variables: dark by default (system- and toggle-overridable, persisted), every hardcoded color in index.html and the cytoscape style mapped through the palette
carry owner and status from node props into viewer data so styles can select on them
statements table added to schema; store grows statement CRUD, dump/load, and the reverse index (statement_references)
works render as boxes of their operations; membership is nesting; only combinators draw as edges; layout is deterministic

props.grounds: headless screenshots of the sandbox viewer, 2026-06-10

nodes.label: endorse endpoint takes statements on and rewrites the views; bad ids and empty payloads are refused

props.grounds: curl against a sandbox server: endorsed, already-owned, missing id, and empty payload paths all answered correctly; work.md re-rendered

nodes.label: the work loop speaks the alphabet: six operation kinds, combinators as relations, roles in props

props.warrant: operator chose Fold it in conversation, 2026-06-10

nodes.label: synthesize and repair stay out until the primitive-vs-compound contest resolves; adopt only if real work keeps wanting them

the operator works in the dark viewer comfortably and watches a change land without refreshing

props.grounds: operator, 2026-06-10, in conversation: the viewer as seen is not yet comfortable to work in — nodes read as undifferentiated colored shapes. No complaint named against the dark theme or live updates specifically; the fail lands on the readability clause.

nodes.label: viewer: machine-owned statements amber and dashed, work nodes diamonds dimmed by status, toolbar counts pending endorsement and open work, details panel names owner and folding condition
less, not more: progressive disclosure — works collapsed by default and expanded on focus, focus-plus-context navigation instead of the whole graph at once
roles land in props with kind defaults; a commit's decide cannot be delegated to the machine

props.grounds: exercised on a sandbox copy of the repo, 2026-06-10: all refusal paths and the full gauntlet ran

nodes.label: style: machine-owned statements amber and dashed; work nodes diamonds, dimmed once folded
viewer v2: intent panel with endorse buttons, works as boxes containing their operations, deterministic layout, zoom controls, statement and operation detail panes

operator chose the three modes of interaction: watch the loop live (operations landing visibly in real time), be summoned to judge (a front-and-center queue of everything awaiting operator judgment), and act in the viewer (verdicts, endorsements, amendments, folds in place). Authoring intent directly in the viewer was offered and not chosen
live loop feed: the existing store fingerprint already re-derives the view; surface what changed — nodes that arrived since the last tick flash and a small feed names the recent moves
work-fold's machine check reads test operations' verdicts instead of check nodes
replace work-add kinds step/candidate/check/result with the six operations; products become material on operation nodes, not node kinds
operator named the failures to fix: two-finger zoom is annoying to navigate, and the screen is a wall of node text, unreadable; statements now arrive beside the graph and need their own panel
the act-in-place endpoints map one-to-one onto the verbs and the gates hold

props.grounds: CDP + curl, 2026-06-10: /api/verdict recorded pass with grounds prefixed 'operator, via the viewer'; /api/amend rewrote the text and took ownership for the operator; /api/strike removed and renumbered; /api/fold without a commit refused with the gate's own words — re-verified against the live server

nodes.label: dark by default, light by toggle: both themes render coherently; the toggle persists and keeps the viewport

props.grounds: headless screenshots of a sandbox serve in both themes, 2026-06-10; CDP toggle test: dark→light flips data-theme, persists to localStorage, zoom unchanged to 1e-9

nodes.label: nodes carry kind, words, and verdict state on the card; works collapse to closed boxes and expand on focus; rows follow the alphabet so the story reads top-down

props.grounds: CDP suite against a sandbox serve, 2026-06-10: 14/14 — collapsed by default (0 operations drawn), focus expands the work, the card label opens with its kind and carries its words, the failed operator test reads '✕ fail' on the card; screenshots of expanded works coherent in both themes

nodes.label: show on a statement answers the reverse direction: bound work, producing work, links both ways, material via the bound work
operator judged the at-a-glance test failed: nodes are still not very useful to see, they are just different colors — a node should carry its meaning visibly, not only its category
relation types carry the combinators: depends-on as causal link, tests, commits, reframes, decomposes-into
statements.json holds all 51 statements with envelope: id, text, owner, segment, ord, links, created_at
one-time migration: 51 statements moved with envelope and 2 depends-on links; spawned-by relations became on/produces props; m_cca715e7 rehomed to w_423ff83b; statement and segment nodes deleted
roles as props on each operation (propose, execute, judge, decide); decide on a commit is the operator's and cannot be delegated
navigation has explicit zoom controls and a gentler wheel

props.grounds: headless screenshots; wheelSensitivity 0.2 plus toolbar buttons

nodes.label: statements live beside the graph: statements table + statements.json, nodes reference them via on/produces props, show answers the reverse index, all verbs and views unchanged

judgment queue: a front-and-center panel listing exactly what awaits the operator — pending endorsements, open operator-judged tests, folds awaiting confirmation — each item jumps to its node and offers the action

act in place: verdict buttons on operator-judged tests, amend and strike on statements, fold confirmation on works — POST endpoints mapping one-to-one onto the verbs, every action recorded with the operator as warrant
work-fold's machine gate reads test verdicts and requires a commit as settlement; render and load round-trip survive

props.grounds: exercised on a sandbox copy of the repo, 2026-06-10: all refusal paths and the full gauntlet ran

nodes.label: the viewer today is read-mostly: one endorse button; every other verb — verdicts, amending, opening and folding work — flows through conversation with the machine. The operator wants to act on the system where they see it
test may want to split into challenge and verdict; decide from practice, not theory

operator named what a node must show at a glance: its words (text readable on the node), its state (verdict, endorsement, open/folded — loud), its place in the story (the causal narrative readable from the picture), and less, not more (progressive disclosure over the whole graph at once)
